title: Number of European overseas colonies and colonizers
subtitle: The colonizers are the United Kingdom, France, Spain, Portugal, Netherlands, Germany, Belgium, and Italy. Only colonized countries that are independent today are considered.
caption: Between 1462 and 2022, the number of independent countries categorized as colonized, colonizer, or neither shifted significantly. The chart tracks the count of European overseas colonies and colonizers, specifically the United Kingdom, France, Spain, Portugal, Netherlands, Germany, Belgium, and Italy. From the late 15th century through the 19th century, the number of colonized territories rose steadily, reaching a peak of approximately 95 around 1900. This period of expansion coincided with a reduction in the number of countries classified as neither colonized nor colonizer. Following the peak in the early 20th century, the number of colonized territories declined sharply, returning to near zero by the 1970s. Throughout the entire timeline, the number of European colonizers remained constant at eight. By the end of the period, the vast majority of independent countries fell into the category of neither colonized nor colonizer.
